jokes.text: Have you even moved since I left this morning? Yes, of course, what are you talking about? I got up to tip room service.
jokes.setup: Cheryl accuses Larry of not moving all day
jokes.punchline: Larry's pathetic example of movement is getting up to tip room service
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - reveals his laziness through the most minimal possible action he can cite as 'movement'

jokes.text: Come on, that's funny. No, it's not funny. It might have been funny a week ago, but enough is enough.
jokes.setup: Larry thinks his lazy behavior is amusing
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's exasperated response that it stopped being funny a week ago
jokes.explanation: Classic dynamic between enabler and exasperated partner, well-executed but not exceptional

jokes.text: Every time I walk in, you're just sitting here... with your chips and your cookies and watching... Maury.
jokes.setup: Cheryl lists Larry's sedentary lifestyle
jokes.punchline: The specificity of watching Maury adds pathetic detail
jokes.explanation: Specific details make this funnier than generic laziness complaints

jokes.text: No, because they're gonna go to boot camp... and I want to see which ones get rehabilitated. They break them down and they cry. And then when they're done with the boot camp... they're all better.
jokes.setup: Larry refuses to get dressed for house hunting
jokes.punchline: His earnest investment in a trashy TV show's rehabilitation storyline
jokes.explanation: Larry's childlike fascination with garbage TV while shirking adult responsibilities is perfectly in character

jokes.text: This is like for the Osmonds, this place.
jokes.setup: Looking at a large house
jokes.punchline: Larry's specific pop culture reference to the large Mormon family
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical reference that captures both the house size and Larry's cultural perspective

jokes.text: When do you use the wake-up calls? You never leave. Naps.
jokes.setup: Larry praising hotel amenities like wake-up calls
jokes.punchline: He uses wake-up calls for naps since he never leaves
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - wake-up calls typically help people get up and go, Larry uses them to time his naps

jokes.text: You get used to that in two days.
jokes.setup: Larry marveling at the ocean view
jokes.punchline: Homeowner casually dismissing one of nature's most spectacular sights
jokes.explanation: Perfect LA wealthy person perspective - taking the extraordinary for granted

jokes.text: You get used to that in two days. Come on in. No, really.
jokes.setup: Beautiful ocean view being shown off
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal that you'd get used to a spectacular view in two days
jokes.explanation: Understatement that deflates the grandeur of an ocean view

jokes.text: Can you shoot the whales from the terrace? 'Cause I like to have blubber for breakfast.
jokes.setup: Woman mentions seeing a whale breach
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual question about shooting whales for breakfast
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Larry response - takes beautiful nature moment and immediately thinks of exploitation

jokes.text: Are these the new suckers?
jokes.setup: Jay Schneider greeting Larry and Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Immediately refers to potential house buyers as 'suckers'
jokes.explanation: Immediate character establishment - Jay as sleazy, inappropriate in front of his wife

jokes.text: It's the classic barnacle.
jokes.setup: Discussing typecasting after a hit show
jokes.punchline: Calling it a 'barnacle' - something that attaches and won't let go
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor for typecasting - barnacles are notorious for being impossible to remove

jokes.text: I've got ideas, but I choose not to carry them out.
jokes.setup: Jay asking what Larry's been up to
jokes.punchline: Larry's pretentious way of describing doing nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry pomposity - making deliberate inaction sound philosophical

jokes.text: It's not for sale.
jokes.setup: Larry commenting on Jay's cashmere sweater
jokes.punchline: Jay's defensive response implying Larry wants to buy it
jokes.explanation: Mildly absurd defensive response to a simple compliment

jokes.text: There's no way that sweater's 100% cashmere. It's, like, maybe 35, 40, 50 at the most, but 100?
jokes.setup: Jay claimed his sweater was 100% cashmere
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate fixation on the fabric percentage
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry obsession over trivial details that others would ignore

jokes.text: What kind of person lies... about a percentage of cashmere in his sweater?
jokes.setup: Larry's suspicion about the sweater material
jokes.punchline: Escalating to question the man's entire moral character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - small lie reveals fundamental character flaw

jokes.text: I'm married, that's plenty of work.
jokes.setup: Jay saying Larry needs to work
jokes.punchline: Larry claiming marriage is work enough
jokes.explanation: Fairly standard married-guy humor but delivered with Larry's specific laziness

jokes.text: Is he a high-maintenance guy? What, are you kidding?
jokes.setup: Jay asking if Richard Lewis is high-maintenance
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous response implying it's obvious
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Larry's blunt honesty about his friend

jokes.text: Very happy, and the Prius is extremely happy with me.
jokes.setup: Car dealer asking if Larry's happy with his Prius
jokes.punchline: Larry anthropomorphizing the car as reciprocally happy
jokes.explanation: Clever anthropomorphization that's just absurd enough to be funny

jokes.text: It takes years to be a car salesman.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about becoming a car salesman
jokes.punchline: Tom treating car sales like it requires extensive training
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny professional pomposity about a stereotypically low-skill job

jokes.text: Except women into sex, that's one thing I can't do.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming he can talk anyone into anything
jokes.punchline: His one exception is talking women into sex
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that fits Larry's neurotic character

jokes.text: I guarantee you... l will sell more cars than anybody in your showroom.
jokes.setup: Tom saying Larry won't sell a car on his first day
jokes.punchline: Larry's grandiose counter-claim about outselling everyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry arrogance - completely unjustified confidence

jokes.text: I'm selling cars.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asking if Larry got a job
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact announcement of his bizarre career choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurd information - two words that upend everything

jokes.text: And we have, Corolla, Canola... and Rave.
jokes.setup: Larry practicing his car sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Getting car model names wrong, including 'Canola' (the oil)
jokes.explanation: Good malapropism humor showing Larry's complete ignorance of cars

jokes.text: Some people, they run in marathons, they want to see if they can do that. Others, they swim the English Channel. All I want is work in a showroom for a while.
jokes.setup: Larry defending his car-selling ambition
jokes.punchline: Comparing car sales to epic athletic achievements
jokes.explanation: Brilliant false equivalency - treating mundane work as heroic challenge

jokes.text: And then on Monday when I come home from work... I'll help with the pictures... But I'll be a little tired, too, probably.
jokes.setup: Larry promising to help Cheryl after his first day
jokes.punchline: Already making excuses about being tired from his fake job
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Larry already treating his whim like real labor.

jokes.text: There wasn't an opening.
jokes.setup: Jason asking why Larry didn't say hello to Alan on the phone
jokes.punchline: Larry's overly formal excuse for not interrupting
jokes.explanation: Larry's social awkwardness presented as procedural formality

jokes.text: So what, I ate an éclair out of a garbage can! And, masturbation contests, four people agreeing... l was in a contest and you know I was in it.
jokes.setup: Jason complaining about George's embarrassing behavior
jokes.punchline: Larry revealing he did all those things in real life
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-moment revealing Larry as the real-life inspiration for George's most absurd behaviors

jokes.text: So, l'm a schmuck for being in a masturbation contest?
jokes.setup: Larry's defensive response about the masturbation contest
jokes.punchline: His indignant tone as if it's a reasonable activity to defend
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - defending the indefensible with righteous indignation

jokes.text: And he becomes embittered and grows to hate the character. That's pretty funny. You son of a bitch, that's pretty damn good.
jokes.setup: Larry pitching a show about an actor hating his famous character
jokes.punchline: Jason's realization it's actually a good (and personal) idea
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor that becomes the premise for the later HBO show 'Curb Your Enthusiasm'

jokes.text: I'm selling cars. I start on Monday.
jokes.setup: Jason asking what Larry's working on
jokes.punchline: Larry's deadpan delivery of his absurd new career
jokes.explanation: Great callback that gets funnier each time - the absurdity compounds

jokes.text: Big stuff, big charging, crazy pistons, nutty pistons.
jokes.setup: Customer asking what's in the car engine
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely non-technical nonsense response
jokes.explanation: Perfect car salesman parody - using meaningless adjectives instead of actual information

jokes.text: This is a fucking work of art.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to sell a car
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic assessment of a Toyota Camry
jokes.explanation: Larry's hyperbolic overselling of an ordinary car

jokes.text: You don't have the need to tilt in this car.
jokes.setup: Customer asking about tilt-wheel steering
jokes.punchline: Larry's nonsensical claim that the car eliminates the need to tilt
jokes.explanation: Absurd sales logic - making a missing feature sound like a benefit

jokes.text: Depending on the city, of course. Duluth is a city, it's considered a city... but it's not as big as Brooklyn or whatever.
jokes.setup: Customer asking about gas mileage in the city
jokes.punchline: Larry's rambling clarification about what constitutes a city
jokes.explanation: Larry's tendency to over-explain simple concepts in confusing ways

jokes.text: It's a thick base, it's a thick wheel base.
jokes.setup: Customer asking about wheel base
jokes.punchline: Larry's non-technical description emphasizing 'thickness'
jokes.explanation: More car salesman parody - using meaningless descriptors

jokes.text: That's my middle name, Larry Dana David. That's my step-brother's name, Apheon.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming every name he hears is somehow related to him
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous claim about having a step-brother named 'Apheon'
jokes.explanation: Running gag of Larry claiming false personal connections, escalating to absurd names

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing over here? You're fucking up my shit.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to help another salesman
jokes.punchline: The salesman's aggressive territorial response
jokes.explanation: Standard workplace territorial behavior, mildly funny

jokes.text: I'm the co-creator of the show. No, it's true. Please, buy one, please?
jokes.setup: Larry failing as a car salesman
jokes.punchline: Desperately using his Seinfeld credentials and begging
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - successful person reduced to desperate pleading

jokes.text: What's a house sound? You know, noises that a house makes? Houses make noises?
jokes.setup: Mysterious noise in their new house
jokes.punchline: Larry's incredulous questioning of the concept of house sounds
jokes.explanation: Larry's inability to accept normal homeowner experiences

jokes.text: Told you I didn't trust that Schneider. He didn't say anything about a house sound.
jokes.setup: The house making mysterious noises
jokes.punchline: Larry connecting it back to his earlier suspicions about the cashmere sweater
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that validates Larry's earlier paranoia - the cashmere lie predicted this deception

jokes.text: What are you, fucking Willy Loman? What are you doing here?
jokes.setup: Richard finding Larry actually working as a car salesman
jokes.punchline: Reference to Death of a Salesman's tragic protagonist
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literary reference that elevates Larry's pathetic situation to tragic comedy

jokes.text: Is that 100% cashmere? Would you mind if I check the label?
jokes.setup: Richard wearing a cashmere sweater
jokes.punchline: Larry's obsession manifesting as physical investigation
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the cashmere obsession - Larry can't let it go

jokes.text: Is that a cashmere sweater? This guy had the same one. Of course it's a cashmere sweater. Is that 100% cashmere?
jokes.setup: Larry notices Richard's sweater
jokes.punchline: Immediately returning to the cashmere obsession despite the chaos
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback showing Larry's inability to let go of minor obsessions even in crisis

jokes.text: That's it, Larry, you're fired. Happy?
jokes.setup: Larry's car-selling career falling apart
jokes.punchline: Getting fired after one day, with Richard's sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Satisfying payoff to Larry's overconfidence about car selling

jokes.text: I got fired. You did? I got the boot! Why'd you get fired? I got 86ed.
jokes.setup: Larry coming home early from his car job
jokes.punchline: His enthusiastic use of multiple slang terms for being fired
jokes.explanation: Larry's oddly cheerful delivery of his employment failure

jokes.text: Yeah, if it's trouble... He just says he has to call and get a sitter. That's no problem, I can just do that. It makes perfect sense to me.
jokes.setup: Repairman needing to come back at night for house inspection
jokes.punchline: Larry's oblivious enthusiasm for the inappropriate arrangement
jokes.explanation: Larry's social cluelessness about appropriate repair visit arrangements

jokes.text: So what? You get like an hour dispensation for good news. Who told me that? It's common knowledge.
jokes.setup: Cheryl saying it's too late to call Jason at 10:45
jokes.punchline: Larry's invented rule about late-night calling dispensations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry behavior - inventing social rules that benefit him and claiming they're universal
